frameworks.name: cognitive map
frameworks.one_line: spatial representations serve as a scaffold for organising abstract conceptual structure

frameworks.name: relational generalisation
frameworks.one_line: invariance learning extended from physical space to nonspatial feature manifolds via rotation

core_question: Does physical space serve as a scaffold that enables rotational generalisation of abstract relational concepts to nonspatial domains?
key_assumptions: concepts can be represented as transition vectors on a 2D manifold
rotational invariance is a useful test of abstract generalisation
spatial representations are privileged for relational structure
manipulations: stimulus domain (physical space vs nonspatial feature manifold)
pre-training mapping auditory or visual features to spatial locations
rotation of transition vectors at test
key_findings: humans generalise to rotated exemplars in physical space but not on nonspatial feature manifolds
pre-training feature-to-location mappings enables rotational generalisation in nonspatial domains
interpretations: space functions as a scaffold for learning abstract conceptual invariances
